claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 19 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

First, let me read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have a child – Alex – who's 19 years old and lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 19 years old, I need to ask about their education or training status, as this is important for eligibility.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education. He's doing his A-levels at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. A-levels count as approved education. 

Now I need to ask: do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Does Alex live with you in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. One more important question: is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:
- You live in the UK
- Alex is 19 years old and lives with you
- Alex is in approved education (doing A-levels)
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- You are responsible for Alex

According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. However, you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex is 19 and doing A-levels, which is approved education, you meet this requirement.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the eligibility requirements:
- You're responsible for Alex and live together in the UK
- Although Alex is 19 (over 16), you still qualify because Alex is under 20 and in approved education (A-levels)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

Please note: If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you can still claim the benefit.

Also, remember that your Child Benefit will stop if Alex:
- Finishes their A-levels and doesn't continue in approved education or training
- Turns 20
- Starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education
- Starts getting certain benefits in their own right

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 19 years old and living with the claimant in the UK. According to the eligibility rules, while Child Benefit normally covers children under 16, eligibility extends to children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Alex is doing A-levels, which is approved education. The claimant is responsible for Alex, lives in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 19-year-old child Alex, who is living at home and in approved education (A-levels). The eligibility criteria are met as Alex is under 20, in approved education, living with the claimant in the UK, and no one else is claiming for them.
